description_long: This is a military-style, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined olive green fuselage and red star insignias on the wings and tail, reminiscent of Soviet World War II aircraft. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, with a slight dihedral angle, and the tail features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, positioned just behind the engine cowling, and appears to be a single-seat configuration. The nose is painted red with a black propeller, and the landing gear is of the taildragger type, with two main wheels under the wings and a small tail wheel. The airplane's surface is smooth and metallic, with some matte-painted areas and visible panel lines. The overall design suggests a robust, maneuverable aircraft suitable for low-altitude operations.
description: A green, single-propeller military airplane with red star insignias, mid-mounted straight wings, and a conventional tail. It features an enclosed cockpit, taildragger landing gear, and a streamlined, metallic body.
description_view_0: Side view showing the olive green fuselage, red star insignias, single propeller, and the full profile of the cockpit and tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the straight wingspan, tail structure, and the alignment of the landing gear.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with clear visibility of the propeller, cockpit, and tail details.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the red nose, black propeller, cockpit canopy, and the symmetrical wing and landing gear arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with slight dihedral angle and no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
